system_under_test.name: FraudLens
system_under_test.configuration_notes: PAID mode uses Gemini Vision API for AI detection layer in addition to intrinsic forensic detectors. FREE mode uses intrinsic detectors only.

level_1.notes: 96/96 AI-generated passports correctly identified. Average confidence: 75%. Detectors: PRNU, FFT, GAN fingerprint, metadata integrity.

level_2.notes: 94 Mexican AI-generated passports: 100% detection, 74% average confidence (PAID). 10 Japanese AI-generated passports: 100% detection, 71.6% average confidence (PAID). Key finding: Claude 3 Haiku vision AI classified all as genuine with 90-100% confidence. FFT forensic analysis detected all.

level_3.notes: Screenshot Attack corpus in preparation. Expected Q2 2026.

false_positive_rate.notes: Genuine document control set evaluation in preparation.

compound_attack_rate.note: v1.1 preparation field; A2A instrumentation not enabled in this run

reference_system_comparison.notes: Claude 3 Haiku used as vision AI baseline. Classified 100% of AI-generated passports as genuine with 90-100% confidence. This demonstrates the gap between vision AI and forensic physics approaches.

primary: FFT spectral analysis — frequency domain noise patterns inconsistent with physical camera sensors
GAN fingerprint detection — MidJourney signature patterns
Metadata structural integrity — EXIF field order and byte-level consistency
detection_signals.contradiction_detection: When vision AI returns 'genuine' verdict but intrinsic forensic score exceeds threshold, EXIF-AI contradiction flag is raised, increasing overall fraud score by approximately 8 percentage points

notes: This is the first public SDB-26 evaluation. FraudLens is the reference implementation for SDB-26 and the author of the benchmark. Level 3 and genuine document control set results to be published Q2 2026. We invite other organisations to publish their SDB-26 results.
citation: Mishyn, R. (2026). SDB-26: The 2026 Synthetic Document Benchmark. https://github.com/sevrusik/SDB-26
